patch.value: auto-release.yml workflow_dispatch silently overrides explicit push=false to true

Found 2026-05-17 while dry-running auto-release after the close --reason fix. The workflow's ternary `${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.push || 'true' }}` evaluates as: when push=false, `&& false` short-circuits to `false`, then `|| 'true'` falls through to the cron default 'true'. Result: an operator can never run a true dry-run, because INPUT_PUSH stays 'true' and the workflow's own dry_run+push guard exits 2.

Fix shipped this session: nest a coercion ternary so `inputs.push && 'true' || 'false'` yields a string first, before the outer fallback runs. Same fix applied to dry_run and allow_same_day_release.

Cron path is unaffected (it never enters the workflow_dispatch branch), but operators can now dispatch with dry_run=true push=false to validate the pipeline without making a real release.

author: codex-agent

patch.value: Completed auto-release.yml workflow_dispatch silently overrides explicit push=false to true and closed with verified delivery evidence.
Expected result: closure includes explicit resolution metadata and verifiable delivery evidence.
Actual result: Completed auto-release.yml workflow_dispatch silently overrides explicit push=false to true and closed with verified delivery evidence.
